title: This is for anyone heading into laparoscopic surgery for endo, or tho...
description: This is for anyone heading into laparoscopic surgery for endo, or those currently on the waiting list. We recently had the chance to chat with Liz Bruen, a private Endometriosis Clinical Nurse  Specialist in Wales. What she shared about the laparoscopy process was absolutely mind-blowing. If you would like to speak to Liz yourself, you can find prices, availability and book a zoom appointment with her directly by visiting the link in our bio!

title: The pain is real, it's debilitating, and it deserves to be talked abo...
description: The pain is real, it's debilitating, and it deserves to be talked about. But there's another symptom that affects more than half of people with endo, one that gets dismissed, overlooked, and rarely shows up in the conversation. It's not straightforward, it's not the same for everyone, and understanding it could change the way you talk to your doctor.

title: Always ask for your surgical photos after a laparoscopy.  Always. Tho...
description: Always ask for your surgical photos after a laparoscopy.  Always. Those images are your medical records and you are entitled to them, but most people don't know to ask.  If your results came back clear but something still doesn't feel right, a specialist can review those photos and often spot what was missed.  Endometriosis doesn't always look the way surgeons expect, and subtle lesions are easily overlooked without the right training.  Your photos could be the difference between years more of fighting, and finally getting answers.  Liz covers exactly this in her virtual clinic - link in bio to book.

title: Your MRI is only as useful as the person reading it. For endo, that m...
description: Your MRI is only as useful as the person reading it. For endo, that matters more than most people reglize.  #endometriosis #endoawareness

title: A 30-minute laparoscopy that found nothing doesn't mean nothing is th...
description: A 30-minute laparoscopy that found nothing doesn't mean nothing is there.  A diagnostic laparoscopy is only as good as the person performing it, and not every surgeon is trained to spot every form of endometriosis. Endometriosis nurse Liz breaks down exactly why that happens, and shares something really important that every single person should do after their surgery: ask for your photos. Those images could make all the difference if you ever need a second opinion. Watch before your next appointment.

title: Replying to @Alice O'Neil
description: Replying to @Alice O'Neil

title: Responding to a question from today: Can someone explain why excision...
description: Responding to a question from today: Can someone explain why excision, not ablation? Short answer: Surface level isn't enough. Liz Bruen (Endometriosis CNS & Surgical Assistant) explains that ablation is just "skimming." Treating what’s on the surface is only half the battle.  If you want to understand why endometriosis symptoms often "re-surface" after surgery, listen to this.

title: Excision, not ablation.  Liz Bruen, endometriosis CNS, explains why t...
description: Excision, not ablation.  Liz Bruen, endometriosis CNS, explains why this matters for your laparoscopy. She runs her own virtual clinic - link in bio.

title: Before your laparoscopy, there are questions you should be asking you...
description: Before your laparoscopy, there are questions you should be asking your surgeon and most people don't know what they are.  Liz covers everything, including what your surgeon should be looking at and why asking for photos or a video afterwards is completely reasonable.  Want more personalised advice?  Liz runs a virtual clinic and you can book a session via the link in our bio.

title: Laparoscopy isn't always the only optionm especially for teenagers. P...
description: Laparoscopy isn't always the only optionm especially for teenagers. Pain management alternatives exist, like nerve blocks - a local gngesthetis injection that can be really effective and last up to 6 months. Surgery is not always the first or only step. There are other ways to manage endometriosis pain.   Share with someone who needs to know this.

title: Struggling at work with endometriosis? There's support for that Endom...
description: Struggling at work with endometriosis? There's support for that Endometriosis UK has an employer resource to help you advocate for accommodations like: 💛The right chair 💛Working from home on bad days 💛A parking spot closer to the office 💛Rest breaks 💛Support around fatigue and nutrition It's worth knowing this exists.

title: If you've been told you have bowel endometriosis, this is worth under...
description: If you've been told you have bowel endometriosis, this is worth understanding. There are different levels of bowel involvement and they are not all treated the same way. Most cases are treated with shaving, which means removing endo from the surface of the bowel. It is actually rare for endo to go all the way through the bowel wall, but when it does, a colorectal surgeon needs to be involved. Knowledge is power 💛

title: This one is for everyone dealing with endo belly 💕 #endometriosis #en...
description: This one is for everyone dealing with endo belly 💕 #endometriosis #endobelly

title: It was surprising to us how few people know about enda nurses and the...
description: It was surprising to us how few people know about enda nurses and the ways they can help. We recently had the chance to chat with Liz Bruen, a private Endometriosis Clinical Nurse Specialist in Wales.  She shared some deep insights into the pain points of the system, the reality for women suffering with ends, and the significant toll it takes on mental health. Watch this space, we'll be sharing much more from our conversation soon! For those of you who have had the opportunity to work with an endo nurse: What was your experience like? Was it difficult to get access to one?

title: You have approximately 8 minutes to tell your GP what's wrong, but ho...
description: You have approximately 8 minutes to tell your GP what's wrong, but how do you get it right? None of us enjoy going to the doctor, and the time pressure makes things worse.  You have one chance to explain your problem properly, otherwise you risk being dismissed. So what makes the difference?  Details. Make notes about your symptoms and be as specific as you can. If you're not sure where to start, we can help. We specialise in supporting patients with endometriosis, so if you're struggling to put together clear notes before your next appointment, just drop us a WhatsApp message. Link in bio.

title: There are 3 organisations with support and information specifically f...
description: There are 3 organisations with support and information specifically for teens: 💛The Menstrual Project 💛Endometriosis UK 💛The Endometriosis Foundation Save this and share it with anyone who needs it

title: Painful periods are often one of the first and most well-known sympto...
description: Painful periods are often one of the first and most well-known symptoms of endometriosis. But it doesn't stop there. It will progress. This is not just for those already diagnosed with endo, this is for parents, teachers and classmates too.  Let's spread the gwarnes about Endometriosis. Liz Bruen was the first endometriosis nurse in Wales.  She has now moved to a private virtual space, so if you want to book a consultation with her, go to the link in our bio.

title: A negative MRI does NOT mean you're in the clear! MRI is powerful, es...
description: A negative MRI does NOT mean you're in the clear! MRI is powerful, especially for endometriosis affecting the bowel, appendix, or pouch of Douglas, but it doesn't always correlate with what's actually found during surgery. Sometimes a two-stage procedure is needed simply because imaging didn't capture the full picture. Your symptoms are valid even when your scan says otherwise.

title: It was surprising to us how few people know about endo nurses and the...
description: It was surprising to us how few people know about endo nurses and the ways they can help. We recently had the chance to chat with Liz Bruen, a private Endometriosis Clinical Nurse Specialist in Wales. She shared some deep insights into the pain points of the system, the reality for women suffering with endo, and the significant toll it takes on mental health. She runs her own virtual clinic so if you need a support, check the link in Bio

title: It's a common misconception that an MRI provides a definitive yes or ...
description: It's a common misconception that an MRI provides a definitive yes or no for endometriosis. If your scan comes back clear, it does not mean the pain isn't real, nor does it mean your symptoms are imagined. Equally, an MRI is not a useless tool, it's just a piece of the puzzle. We've put together a guide breaking down exactly what an MRI can (and can't) detect, so you can feel more confident and informed heading into your next appointment.

title: Replying to @user8575620225727  Many of you have asked about the MRI
description: Replying to @user8575620225727  Many of you have asked about the MRI

title: Endometriosis isn't just about painful periods. Chronic fatigue is on...
description: Endometriosis isn't just about painful periods. Chronic fatigue is one of its most common symptoms, and one of the most dismissed, because fatigue can have so many causes, it's rarely the first thing that points doctors toward endometriosis. If you're exhausted in ways that feel bigger than your cycle, it's worth investigating. Liz is an Endometriosis Clinical Nurse Specialist who offers online consultations. Anyone needing help navigating this, she's your person. Link in bio 🔗

title: Different pill. Different patch. Same answer, every time. Then the su...
description: Different pill. Different patch. Same answer, every time. Then the surgery found stage 4 endometriosis.

title: You asked, Liz answered! Can endometriosis be over or under-diagnosed...
description: You asked, Liz answered! Can endometriosis be over or under-diagnosed on MRI? Our endo CNS breaks it down.

title: Did you know that if you have endometriosis, you're eligible for a Hi...
description: Did you know that if you have endometriosis, you're eligible for a Hidden Disabilities Sunflower lanyard? @Laura | Women’s Health Creator shares her experience of wearing one and how it's made a real difference when travelling and on days when a flare-up hits. Because nobody should have to suffer in silence or feel too embarrassed to ask for help. The lanyard is free and there are no eligibility requirements, you simply request one. If you think it could help you, head to hdsunflower.com/uk to find out more.

title: If you're not sure your employer will support you with endometriosis,...
description: If you're not sure your employer will support you with endometriosis,here's some reassurance. Many companies, through HR, will recognise your symptoms and adapt your work to accommodate them. It's always worth having the conversation.

title: A transvaginal scan for endo is not just a regular ultrasound. If you...
description: A transvaginal scan for endo is not just a regular ultrasound. If you have questions about your scan results or diagnosis, link in bio to book a consultation.

title: 9 years. That's how long it takes, on average, to get an endometriosi...
description: 9 years. That's how long it takes, on average, to get an endometriosis diagnosis in the UK. And as much as the system needs to change, it's not the only reason. Awareness, language, being dismissed, not knowing what's "normal".  It all plays a part. Liz Bruen, Endometriosis Clinical Nurse Specialist with 20 years of experience, breaks down why the delay happens, and what you can actually do about it. Liz offers virtual consultations where she'll go through your full symptom history, help you understand your condition, and put together a care plan tailored to you. If you've been waiting, going in circles, or just need someone who actually gets it, her link is in bio.

title: Did you know you can see an endometriosis Clinical Nurse Specialist (...
description: Did you know you can see an endometriosis Clinical Nurse Specialist (CNS) online? Meet Liz Bruen, one of our CNS specialists with over 20 years of experience in endometriosis care. Book your appointment with her, link in bio.

title: This is why early symptoms should never be brushed off. Deeply infilt...
description: This is why early symptoms should never be brushed off. Deeply infiltrating endometriosis can involve a defect in the rectum, often alongside bowel symptoms and it can start as early as age 11. If your daughter is struggling and you want answers, Liz runs a virtual endo clinic, available privately from anywhere in the UK. Link in bio to book

title: An endometriosis nurse, or Clinical Nurse Specialist (CNS), is someon...
description: An endometriosis nurse, or Clinical Nurse Specialist (CNS), is someone like a diabetic nurse - a dedicated specialist who supports patients in managing a complex, long-term condition. They offer guidance on: symptoms  treatments pain management  and emotional wellbeing. Acting as a vital link between patients and their wider medical team. Yet many people, especially those living with endometriosis, have no idea this kind of support even exists. We sat down with Liz to find out exactly what a CNS nurse does, how they can help, how the role came about, and what her virtual clinic looks like in practice. Got a question? Leave it in the comments and Liz will be happy to answer.

title: Replying to @KLF2511 why your pain after the endometriosis surgery mi...
description: Replying to @KLF2511 why your pain after the endometriosis surgery might get worse.

title: If you are hitting a wall getting a diagnosis for endometriosis, this...
description: If you are hitting a wall getting a diagnosis for endometriosis, this one is for you. The best thing you can do is map out your symptoms. What is cyclical, what is not. Details like:  this stops me from going to work or school or I'm passing out on day 1. Even if you think it's not important, write it down. And if you need help figuring out who to see privately or book a session with Liz - link in bio.

title: Periods can be painful, but if you need strong painkillers to be able...
description: Periods can be painful, but if you need strong painkillers to be able to function? GET HELP! Painful periods are often one of the first and most well-known symptoms of endometriosis. But it doesn't stop there. It will progress. This is not just for those already diagnosed with endo, this is for parents, teachers and classmates too. Let's spread the awarnes about Endometriosis.  Liz Bruen was the first endometriosis nurse in Wales. She has now moved to a private virtual space, so if you want to book a consultation with her, go to the link in our bio.

title: Living with endometriosis is exhausting enough. Our endometriosis nur...
description: Living with endometriosis is exhausting enough. Our endometriosis nurse Liz has some thoughts on what's actually helping and what might be actually harmful to you. If you need extra support, she runs a virtual clinic for anyone who wants real, one-on-one guidance from someone who is in the field for the past 20 years. Link in bio

title: 8 minutes. That's sometimes all you get with your GP. If you think yo...
description: 8 minutes. That's sometimes all you get with your GP. If you think you have endometriosis, that's nowhere near enough time to explain everything you're feeling. If you need help to get ready for your appointment, send us a WhatsApp message and we'll help you organise your notes, symptoms and questions so you walk in prepared and nothing gets left out. Your data stays in WhatsApp, nothing is stored or shared. Ever.

title: We all have bad periods, you just have to get used to it. Ayla first ...
description: We all have bad periods, you just have to get used to it. Ayla first started experiencing symptoms at 11 years old. It took years before anyone: took her seriously. This is what endo looks like in teenagers and why we need to do better

title: Painful intercourse is one of the most common symptoms of endometrios...
description: Painful intercourse is one of the most common symptoms of endometriosis and one of the most dismissed. If your GP has brushed it off, you're not alone. The difference between being heard and being sent: home is often in the details, how it impacts your daily life, your relationships, your work. The more specific you are, the harder it is to ignore..

title: Dismissed. Pushed away.  Told it was just period pain. We sat down wi...
description: Dismissed. Pushed away.  Told it was just period pain. We sat down with @Laura | Women’s Health Creator - and founder of @Your Endo Events and covered everything from life with endometriosis, running a community and support group, to what she wishes she knew when she was still searching for answers. We'll be sharing it all soon, stay tuned. What were your first symptoms?

title: It's not all doom and gloom. Researchers are now describing endometri...
description: It's not all doom and gloom. Researchers are now describing endometriosis as a "cancer-mimicking" disease. Meaning it shares biological features with cancer in the way it grows, invades tissue, and resists treatment. This isn't just terminology. It's proof that people are actively working to change how this condition is understood and taken seriously. Liz is an Endometriosis Clinical Nurse Specialist based in Wales, offering online consultations. If you want to speak to her, link in bio.

title: @Laura | Women’s Health Creator struggled with her own endometriosis ...
description: @Laura | Women’s Health Creator struggled with her own endometriosis symptoms and found herself building a community around it on TikTok. Now there are actual events. @Your Endo Events is a community project that grew from that exact moment, and if you're free on the 13th of June, the first ever London event is happening. Worth checking out 💛

title: Replying to @amorphousblob
description: Replying to @amorphousblob

title: Why does it take 9 years on average to get an endometriosis diagnosis...
description: Why does it take 9 years on average to get an endometriosis diagnosis in the UK? It's a simple question with a complicated answer, and Liz breaks it all down in part two. Liz Bruen is an Endometriosis Clinical Nurse Specialist with 20 years of experience and you can book a virtual consultation with her directly.

title: Replying to @celine forrest
description: Replying to @celine forrest

title: We had another chat with Liz to answer some of your questions about t...
description: We had another chat with Liz to answer some of your questions about the 360-degree assessment. So many of you had follow-up questions, and while we tried to reply to as many as we could, some needed Liz and her expertise to answer properly. We'll be sharing more soon, so stay tuned, and if you want to book an appointment with Liz, link in bio. Answering question from @Poppy Alice Stepto

title: Do you document your endo symptoms? @Laura | Women’s Health Creator l...
description: Do you document your endo symptoms? @Laura | Women’s Health Creator learned that writing everything down - bowel changes, pain levels, how her cycle affected her day - gave her the clarity and confidence to advocate for herself with every GP and specialist she saw. Your symptoms deserve to be taken seriously. Documenting them is how you make sure they are. Need help organising your notes before an appointment? We can help! Chat to us on WhatsApp.

title: Endometriosis takes. It takes your time, your energy, and life as you...
description: Endometriosis takes. It takes your time, your energy, and life as you knew it. But it doesn't have to be the end of the story. @Laura | Women’s Health Creator is living proof that struggle can be transformed into something powerful. She turied her experience with endometriosis into advocacy, content creation, and a nonprofit events, building a community that helps countless women navigate the same road she once walked alone. You are not alone and you don't have to stay that way. Check out @Your Endo Events - a London meetup is coming soon.

title: Ayla had a diagnosis on her notes.  She was still sent home and told ...
description: Ayla had a diagnosis on her notes.  She was still sent home and told she was too young. This isn't just a medical problem.  It's an awareness problem.  And it starts with conversations like this one.

title: Al can be a powerful tool when navigating endometriosis, if you know ...
description: Al can be a powerful tool when navigating endometriosis, if you know how to use it. @Laura | Women’s Health Creator shared how she used Al to make sense of her medical records, understand her MRI results, and ask the right questions to get referred to a specialist who finally helped her manage her endometriosis symptoms. But you don't have to figure it out alone. We can help you navigate the system, understanding your symptoms and finding the right private specialist for where you live. Just come and talk to us. We'll help you ask the right questions. Link in BlO.

title: This is for anyone heading into laparoscopic surgery for endo, or tho...
description: This is for anyone heading into laparoscopic surgery for endo, or those currently on the waiting list. We recently had the chance to chat with Liz Bruen, a private Endometriosis Clinical Nurse Specialist in Wales. What she shared about the laparoscopy process was absolutely mind-blowing. If you would like visit Liz's virtual clinic yourself, you can find prices, availability and book a zoom appointment with her directly. Link in our bio!

title: We’ll put you on the pill, that will sort you out. This is just your ...
description: We’ll put you on the pill, that will sort you out. This is just your first period, wait until it settles. Ayla was only 11 years old when her symptoms started. Despite experiencing severe pain, it took 4 years of being dismissed before she finally received her endometriosis diagnosis. This is what endometriosis looks like in teens.  It’s not just a bad period, and it doesn’t just affect adults.

title: This is a waste of time. Stage 4 endometriosis said otherwise🎗️ Endom...
description: This is a waste of time. Stage 4 endometriosis said otherwise🎗️ Endometriosis symptoms can start as early as childhood. In Ayla's case, it started when she was 11 years old.

title: Did you know you can see an endometriosis Clinical Nurse Specialist (...
description: Did you know you can see an endometriosis Clinical Nurse Specialist (CNS) online? Meet Liz Bruen, one of our CNS specialists with over 20 years of experience in endometriosis care. Book your appointment with her, link in bio.

title: Don't to be silly. You are too young. This is an adult disease. If th...
description: Don't to be silly. You are too young. This is an adult disease. If this hits close to home, you are not alone. So many of teen girls walked into doctor's offices with questions and walked out feeling defeated. @ENDOSIS | Ayla has had endometriosis since 11, and this is her story.

title: 9 years. That's how long it takes, on average, to get an endometriosi...
description: 9 years. That's how long it takes, on average, to get an endometriosis diagnosis in the UK. And it's not just the system. Awareness, dismissed symptoms, pain that gets written off as just a bad period, not knowing what's normal - it all adds up Liz Bruen, Endometriosis Clinical Nurse Specialist with 20 years of experience, breaks down why the delay happens and what you can actually do about it. If you've been waiting, going in circles, or just need someone who gets it.

title: Endo affects 1 in 10 women. The average diagnosis takes 9 years. It's...
description: Endo affects 1 in 10 women. The average diagnosis takes 9 years. It's on the list of the 20 most painful diseases. There is no cure. This is endometriosis. And it's not just a period problem. It's a symptom of something bigger: women's health has been overlooked for centuries. There's even a term for it -bikini medicine - the medical assumption that women are just like men, except for the parts a bikini covers. Everything else? Ignored. We can do better. If you're struggling with endo and don't know where to start, message us on WhatsApp. We're not doctors, but we'll help point you in the right direction. Link in BIO

title: Period pain that ruins your day is not something to push through, it'...
description: Period pain that ruins your day is not something to push through, it's something to investigate. Conditions like endometriosis are more common than you think, and earlier diagnosis changes everything. Save this and share it with someone who needs to hear this 💕

title: Painful intercourse is one of the most common symptoms of endometrios...
description: Painful intercourse is one of the most common symptoms of endometriosis and one of the most dismissed. If your GP has brushed it off, you're not alone. The difference between being heard and being sent home is often in the details, how it impacts your daily life, your relationships, your work. The more specific you are, the harder it is to ignore.

title: Liz, Clinical Nurse Specialist in endometriosis, recommends to visit:...
description: Liz, Clinical Nurse Specialist in endometriosis, recommends to visit: 💛The Menstrual Project 💛Endometriosis UK 💛The Endometriosis Foundation Share with someone who needs it

title: Going into your endometriosis appointment unprepared can mean walking...
description: Going into your endometriosis appointment unprepared can mean walking out without answers. Liz, Clinical Nurse Specialist in endometriosis, recommends using a symptom checker - tracking your symptoms over weeks or months so you have something concrete to bring to your GP or consultant. If you want help preparing for your next appointment, Liz runs a virtual endo clinic, available privately from anywhere in the UK. Link in bio to book.

title: The surgeon wasn't even an endo specialist. She was a general gynecol...
description: The surgeon wasn't even an endo specialist. She was a general gynecologist who didn't believe Ayla had endometriosis at all, and said so, out loud, while Ayla was being sedated. Then she opened her up and found endo everywhere. This isn't a story about one bad doctor.  It's a story about a system where girls have to survive doubt just to get diagnosed, and where "I told you so" comes from inside an operating theatre. Imagine this happening to you, while being just 15.

title: in 10 women has endometriosis. For most, the symptoms started as a te...
description: in 10 women has endometriosis. For most, the symptoms started as a teenager. It's time schools started teaching young girls that debilitating pain is NOT normal. Early education = earlier diagnosis = less suffering.

title: To everyone who was told their post-surgery pain isn't real, we belie...
description: To everyone who was told their post-surgery pain isn't real, we believe you. Endometriosis is complex, and surgery is not always a cure. Your experience is valid, your pain is real, and you deserve proper care 💛

title: No leaflet, no conversation about what stage it was, where it was, or...
description: No leaflet, no conversation about what stage it was, where it was, or what they did. Just a discharge letter. Six months later... This is still happening to women every single day.

title: Work doesn't have to be another battle on top of endometriosis. Most ...
description: Work doesn't have to be another battle on top of endometriosis. Most people don't reglise how much room there is to ask for support and that bringing the right information to that conversation can make all the difference. It's not about asking for special treatment.  It's about asking for what helps you actually function.

title: Unexplained infertility isn't a diagnosis, it's often just where the ...
description: Unexplained infertility isn't a diagnosis, it's often just where the workup stopped looking. If pain has never been part of your story, it's easy to get waved past endometriosis entirely. But some of the most extensive disease causes zero pain - for a lot of people, trouble conceiving is the only sign there ever is. Save this for your next appointment, and share it with someone who needs to see it.

title: Liz is very direct about what symptoms should never be dismissed. Mos...
description: Liz is very direct about what symptoms should never be dismissed. Most endo symptoms start at 11. If you've been dismissed and don't know where to turn you can book a consultation with Liz (BIO)

title: Putting a woman on the pill to 'sort out' her painful periods without...
description: Putting a woman on the pill to 'sort out' her painful periods without a proper diagnosis is not a solution. Ayala was put in the pill when she was just 11. This is exactly how endometriosis has been overlooked and undertregted for decades. Pain is not a hormone problem to be masked. It deserves investigation

title: Endo surgery isn't a cure. It's not do this once and you're done. But...
description: Endo surgery isn't a cure. It's not do this once and you're done. But that's what so many are told.  Ayla learned this when she was 15. She learned that this won’t go away one day, this is a chronic disease. Sharing this for anyone who needs to feel less alone in it.
